title: C# Strings
content:
### Strings in C#

A string is a sequence of characters stored as a `string` type (alias for `System.String`). Strings are immutable in C#.

---

### Creating Strings

```csharp
string str1 = "Hello";
string str2 = "World";
string str3 = new string('x', 5);
```

---

### String Concatenation

```csharp
string fullName = "John" + " " + "Doe";
string message = string.Concat("Hello", " ", "World");
string greeting = $"Hello {name}, you are {age}";
```

---

### Common String Methods

| Method | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `Length` | Character count | `"Hello".Length` = 5 |
| `ToUpper()` | Uppercase | `"hello".ToUpper()` = "HELLO" |
| `ToLower()` | Lowercase | `"HELLO".ToLower()` = "hello" |
| `Trim()` | Remove whitespace | `" hello ".Trim()` = "hello" |
| `Substring()` | Extract part | `"Hello".Substring(1, 3)` = "ell" |
| `Replace()` | Replace chars | `"Hello".Replace('e', 'a')` = "Hallo" |
| `Contains()` | Check substring | `"Hello".Contains("ell")` = true |
| `Split()` | Split into array | `"a,b,c".Split(',')` |
| `Join()` | Join array to string | `string.Join(",", arr)` |

---

### Escape Sequences

```csharp
string quote = "He said \"Hello\"";
string path = "C:\\Users\\Name";
string multiline = "Line1\nLine2";
```

---

### Verbatim Strings

Prefix with `@` to ignore escape sequences:

```csharp
string path = @"C:\Users\Name";
string multi = @"Line1
Line2
Line3";
```

---

### Raw String Literals (C# 11+)

```csharp
string json = """
{
    "name": "John"
}
""";
```

> **Key Point:** Strings are immutable - methods like `ToUpper()` return new strings. Use `StringBuilder` for repeated manipulation.
